meta.title: Historical Events
meta.subtitle: Turning points across world history — searchable.

glossary.heading: Periods — how the historical eras are bounded

terms.Ancient (to 500 CE): From the earliest records through the fall of the western Roman empire.
terms.Medieval (500–1500): The middle ages across Europe, the Islamic world, and Asia.
terms.Early modern (1500–1800): The age of exploration, reformation, and revolutions.
terms.20th century onward: World wars, decolonisation, and the digital age.

meta.footer: A placeholder topic seeded with sample events. Dates are scholarly estimates where exact dates are unknown.

entries.name: Founding of Rome
entries.desc: Traditional date for the founding of the city of Rome, later the centre of the Roman Republic and Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Marathon
entries.desc: Athenian-led victory over the first Persian invasion of Greece, a defining moment for the Greek city-states.

entries.name: Unification of China under Qin
entries.desc: Qin Shi Huang united the warring states into the first centralised Chinese empire.

entries.name: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
entries.desc: Deposition of Romulus Augustulus, conventionally marking the end of the western Roman Empire.

entries.name: The Hijra
entries.desc: Muhammad's migration from Mecca to Medina, the starting point of the Islamic calendar.

entries.name: Norman Conquest of England
entries.desc: William of Normandy's victory at Hastings reshaped English language, law, and aristocracy.

entries.name: Fall of Constantinople
entries.desc: Ottoman capture of the Byzantine capital, often taken to mark the end of the Middle Ages.

entries.name: Columbus Reaches the Americas
entries.desc: First of Columbus's voyages, opening sustained European contact with the Americas.

entries.name: Arrival of the First Fleet
entries.desc: British ships established the first European colony in Australia at Sydney Cove.

entries.name: French Revolution Begins
entries.desc: Storming of the Bastille opened a decade of upheaval that toppled the French monarchy.

entries.name: Meiji Restoration
entries.desc: Restoration of imperial rule in Japan and the start of rapid modernisation.

entries.name: Berlin Conference
entries.desc: European powers formalised the partition and colonisation of Africa.

entries.name: Partition of India
entries.desc: Independence and division of British India into India and Pakistan.

entries.name: Apollo 11 Moon Landing
entries.desc: First crewed landing on the Moon, by the United States' Apollo programme.

entries.name: Siege of Van
entries.desc: Defense of Van (1915), the Armenian population's self-defense against Ottoman forces during the Armenian genocide.

entries.name: Battle of Fahl
entries.desc: Major battle in the Muslim conquest of Byzantine Syria, fought by Rashidun Caliphate forces near Pella (Fahl).

entries.name: Polish–Muscovite War
entries.desc: The Polish–Muscovite War (1605–18), a conflict between the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth and the Tsardom of Russia.

entries.name: Treaty of Hünkâr İskelesi
entries.desc: Treaty between the Russian Empire and the Ottoman Empire establishing a mutual defence alliance.

entries.name: Battle of Madagascar
entries.desc: Allied campaign to capture the Vichy French-controlled island of Madagascar during World War II.

entries.name: Battle of Guandu
entries.desc: Cao Cao's decisive victory over Yuan Shao's larger forces, a turning point of the late Eastern Han dynasty.

entries.name: Battle of Puebla
entries.desc: Mexican victory over French forces at Puebla during the second French intervention in Mexico.

entries.name: Battle of Auray
entries.desc: Decisive confrontation of the Breton War of Succession, part of the Hundred Years' War.

entries.name: Pig War
entries.desc: Largely bloodless 1859 border confrontation between the United States and the British Colony of Vancouver Island.

entries.name: Siege of Vicksburg
entries.desc: Final major military action of the Vicksburg campaign, ending in a decisive Union victory in the American Civil War.

entries.name: Battle of Los Angeles
entries.desc: A rumored 1942 Japanese air attack on Los Angeles triggered an anti-aircraft artillery barrage over the city.

entries.name: Battle of Rivoli
entries.desc: Napoleon's decisive defeat of Austrian forces near Rivoli, the climax of the Italian campaign of 1796–97.

entries.name: King William's War
entries.desc: North American theater of the Nine Years' War, fought between New France and New England and their Native allies.

entries.name: Battle of Smolensk
entries.desc: Battle of Smolensk (1812), fought during Napoleon's invasion of Russia.

entries.name: Battle of Vaslui
entries.desc: Stephen III of Moldavia's victory over Ottoman forces near Vaslui in 1475.

entries.name: Battle of Maipú
entries.desc: Decisive Patriot victory over Spanish royalists near Santiago, completing Chile's independence.

entries.name: Battle of Yamen
entries.desc: Naval battle marking the last stand of the Song dynasty against the invading Yuan dynasty forces.

entries.name: Battle of Neerwinden
entries.desc: Battle of Neerwinden (1793), a French defeat to Austrian forces during the War of the First Coalition.

entries.name: Siege of Bursa
entries.desc: Long Ottoman siege that ended in the capture of Bursa (Prusa) in 1326, an early Ottoman conquest.

entries.name: Battle of Fulford
entries.desc: Harald Hardrada and Tostig Godwinson's defeat of the Northern Earls near York, days before Stamford Bridge.

entries.name: Battle of Worcester
entries.desc: Oliver Cromwell's Parliamentarian army defeated Charles II's Royalist forces at Worcester, ending the Wars of the Three Kingdoms.

entries.name: Union of Krewo
entries.desc: Grand Duke Jogaila of Lithuania pledged to marry Queen Jadwiga of Poland, laying the groundwork for a Polish-Lithuanian union.

entries.name: Battle of Alcácer Quibir
entries.desc: Portuguese forces under King Sebastian were destroyed by a Moroccan army near Ksar-el-Kebir, ending Portugal's independent monarchy.

entries.name: Battle of Rorke's Drift
entries.desc: A small British garrison defended a mission station against a much larger Zulu force during the Anglo-Zulu War.

entries.name: Battle of Mons Graupius
entries.desc: Roman forces under Agricola defeated a Caledonian army in what is now Scotland, per Tacitus's account.

entries.name: Battle of Ad Decimum
entries.desc: Byzantine general Belisarius defeated the Vandal army near Carthage, opening the reconquest of North Africa.

entries.name: Great Molasses Flood
entries.desc: A ruptured storage tank released a wave of molasses through Boston's North End, killing 21 people.

entries.name: Battle of Princeton
entries.desc: George Washington's forces defeated a British rearguard at Princeton, New Jersey, boosting American morale after Trenton.

entries.name: Battle of the Camel
entries.desc: Caliph Ali defeated a rebel coalition led by Aisha, Talha, and Zubayr outside Basra, in the first civil war of Islam.

entries.name: Vandalic War
entries.desc: Byzantine forces under Justinian I reconquered North Africa from the Vandal Kingdom.

entries.name: Battle of Omdurman
entries.desc: Anglo-Egyptian forces under Kitchener crushed the Mahdist army near Omdurman, Sudan, cementing British control of the region.

entries.name: Battle of Mons Lactarius
entries.desc: Byzantine forces defeated the Ostrogoths near Mount Vesuvius, ending organized Gothic resistance in Italy.

entries.name: Battle of Annual
entries.desc: Riffian Berber forces inflicted a catastrophic defeat on the Spanish Army in northeastern Morocco, triggering political crisis in Spain.

entries.name: Smolensk War
entries.desc: The Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth fought Muscovy over control of Smolensk.

entries.name: Battle of Formigny
entries.desc: A decisive French victory over England near Formigny hastened the end of the Hundred Years' War in Normandy.

entries.name: Battle of Wake Island
entries.desc: Japanese forces captured the American-held Pacific atoll of Wake Island after a fierce two-week defense.

entries.name: Battles of Kawanakajima
entries.desc: Takeda Shingen and Uesugi Kenshin fought a series of inconclusive battles for control of the Kawanakajima plain in Sengoku-era Japan.
